inputs.text: Main thaka hua mehsoos kar raha hoon, bukhar bhi hai, sir dard hai

hypotheses.label: Viral fever
hypotheses.rationale: Low-grade fever, malaise, headache are common symptoms of viral fever

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Gastroenteritis can also cause fever, malaise, and headache

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Early symptoms of typhoid are non-specific and can be similar to viral fever

inputs.text: Mera bukhar roz badh raha hai, pet dard bhi ho raha hai, kabz bhi hai

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: Step-wise increase in fever and abdominal pain are suggestive of typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Gastroenteritis can cause abdominal pain and constipation

hypotheses.label: Viral fever
hypotheses.rationale: Viral fever is less likely given the increasing fever and abdominal pain

inputs.text: Ab to bukhar bahut tez ho gaya hai, kabz ke baad dast ho gaya hai, bahut kamzori mehsoos ho rahi hai

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: High fever, diarrhea after constipation, and severe weakness are cardinal signs of typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Gastroenteritis can cause diarrhea, but the combination of symptoms is more suggestive of typhoid

hypotheses.label: Malaria
hypotheses.rationale: Malaria is less likely given the absence of characteristic paroxysms of fever

inputs.text: Ab to main bilkul thaka hua hoon, bukhar abhi bhi hai, khada bhi nahi ho pa raha hoon

hypotheses.label: Typhoid
hypotheses.rationale: The patient's condition has worsened, and the symptoms are consistent with typhoid

hypotheses.label: Gastroenteritis
hypotheses.rationale: Ruled out due to the prolonged and severe nature of the illness

hypotheses.label: Malaria
hypotheses.rationale: Ruled out due to the absence of characteristic symptoms and the patient's worsening condition
